name: Sandwich Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound Confectioners sugar (2 cups loosely packed)
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, beat egg yolks and milk until well blended. Add flour, sugar, butter, vanilla, and salt; beat ingredients until just mixed. Increase speed to medium and beat for 2 minutes, scraping the sides of the bowl occasionally.
Divide dough in half. On a floured pastry cloth, roll one half of the dough 1/8-inch thick using a lightly floured rolling pin. Cut dough into rounds with a 2 1/4-inch-fluted-edged cookie cutter, and cut out centers from half of the rounds with a 1/2-inch round cutter. Reserve the dough scraps.
Using a pancake turner, place the rounds 1/2-inch apart on un-greased cookie sheets and bake at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for 8-10 minutes until lightly browned. Remove cookies to wire racks to cool completely.
Repeat cutting and baking with the second half of the dough, rolling scraps and rerolling as needed.
Prepare frosting: In the bowl of an electric mixer, place all frosting ingredients (except food color) and beat at low speed for 2 minutes. Scrape sides of the bowl and continue beating at high speed for 6 minutes. Divide frosting into thirds and tint with green, red, and yellow food color. On the bottom side of a cookie without a hole, spread a teaspoonful of either colored frosting; top with a cookie with a hole in it to make a "sandwich." Repeat with remaining cookies and frosting.
Yield: about 42 sandwich cookies.
Note: Confectioners sugar is also known as powdered sugar.
